description: Marriage-based green card intake playbook. Covers three scenarios: USC petitioner with adjustment of status (AOS), LPR petitioner, and consular processing (DS-260). Engine reads this spec to drive fact extraction prompts, deterministic gap/evidence/escalation checks, and completeness scoring.
_comment_scenarios: scenario is resolved EARLY from two facts: petitioner_immigration_status (USC vs LPR) and beneficiary_location (inside_us vs abroad). The resolved scenario decides which forms, required_facts, and required_documents apply. Fields tagged with applies_to are only enforced for matching scenarios.

USC_AOS.label: U.S. Citizen petitioner, beneficiary in U.S., Adjustment of Status
USC_AOS.notes: Concurrent filing of I-130 + I-485 permitted. Immediate relative, no visa wait.

LPR_petitioner.label: Lawful Permanent Resident petitioner (F2A preference)
LPR_petitioner.notes: F2A preference category. Visa availability subject to Visa Bulletin; AOS only when priority date current. If beneficiary abroad, route to consular processing.

consular_DS260.label: Beneficiary abroad, consular processing
consular_DS260.notes: After I-130 approval, case goes to NVC then consulate. Medical exam done abroad by panel physician.

_comment_facts: required_facts drive (a) the extraction prompt sent to the model and (b) the follow_up_questions output when missing. priority controls intake question ordering. confirm_by_human=true means even if the model extracts it, it must be human-verified before it counts toward completeness.

required_facts.label: Beneficiary currently inside U.S. or abroad
Do the spouses live together; since when
Address timeline for both spouses (5 yrs)
Prior marriages of either spouse (count + termination)
Beneficiary most recent entry: date, place, manner (I-94 / inspection)
required_facts._note: Lawful entry/inspection is a precondition for AOS. Not required for consular.

required_facts.label: Overstay, prior denials, removal/deportation, unlawful presence
Is F2A priority date current (LPR only)

_comment_docs: required_documents are checked against uploaded/classified document objects. status per doc: provided | missing | needs_supplementation. conditional docs only become required when their condition_fact is satisfied.

identity.label: Petitioner USC proof (passport/naturalization cert) or LPR green card

marriage.label: Divorce decrees / death certificates for prior marriages

financial_support.label: Most recent federal tax return / transcript

civil_documents_consular.label: Police certificates from countries of residence

_comment_evidence: evidence_map is the heart of the magic moment. Each category has a min_items threshold. The deterministic check layer counts matched evidence items per category and compares to min_items. bona_fide_marriage is the most important category for adjudication.
_comment_flags: escalation_flags run as deterministic rules in the check layer. trigger is evaluated against extracted facts + evidence counts. action drives workflow: notify_attorney (info banner), attorney_review_required (gate before finalize), block_petition_assembly (hard stop until resolved). Each fired flag MUST carry a source reference.

escalation_flags.label: Conditional residence (marriage < 2 years at approval)
escalation_flags.message: Marriage under 2 years: beneficiary will receive conditional residence (CR1/CR6). Plan for I-751 within 90 days before the 2-year anniversary.

escalation_flags.label: Bona fide marriage evidence below threshold
escalation_flags.message: Bona fide marriage evidence below minimum. Collect additional joint documents before assembling the petition.

escalation_flags.label: Conflicting addresses or dates
escalation_flags.message: Conflicting address/date information detected. Reconcile before filing.

escalation_flags.label: Criminal history present
escalation_flags.message: Criminal history present. Requires attorney admissibility assessment (possible waiver analysis).

escalation_flags.label: Overstay / prior denial / removal / unlawful presence
escalation_flags.message: Adverse immigration history present. Assess inadmissibility bars and waiver eligibility (e.g., 3/10-year bar, I-601/I-601A).

escalation_flags.label: AOS may be barred by unlawful entry
escalation_flags.message: Last entry without inspection. AOS likely unavailable; assess consular processing + I-601A provisional waiver.

escalation_flags.label: Petitioner income below I-864 guideline
escalation_flags.message: Petitioner income appears below 125% poverty guideline for household size. Joint sponsor or assets likely required.

escalation_flags.label: F2A priority date not current (LPR petitioner)
escalation_flags.message: F2A priority date not current. AOS cannot be filed until visa available per Visa Bulletin.

_comment_scoring: Completeness scoring produces BOTH a dimension breakdown and a single overall score. Dimensions map to the four pillars adjudicators care about. Overall = weighted sum of dimension scores minus flag penalties, floored at 0 and capped at 100. The breakdown is what gets shown to the attorney as the explainable 'magic moment'.
_comment_output: output_contract pins the structure of each deliverable so results are stable, reviewable, and handoff-ready. Each item carries source references back to transcript timestamps or document objects.
cover_letter_template:
[LAW FIRM LETTERHEAD]
[DATE]

U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services
[APPLICABLE USCIS FILING ADDRESS]

RE: Form I-130, Petition for Alien Relative & Form I-485, Application to Register Permanent Residence
Petitioner: [PETITIONER FULL LEGAL NAME]
Beneficiary/Applicant: [BENEFICIARY FULL LEGAL NAME]
Case Type: Marriage-Based Green Card (Immediate Relative)

Dear USCIS Officer:

This letter is submitted in support of the concurrently filed Form I-130 Petition for Alien Relative and Form I-485 Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status filed on behalf of [BENEFICIARY NAME] by their U.S. Citizen spouse, [PETITIONER NAME].

The enclosed documentation demonstrates that the Petitioner and Beneficiary entered into a bona fide marriage in good faith, and that the Beneficiary is eligible for adjustment of status.

I. FACTUAL BACKGROUND
[PETITIONER NAME] and [BENEFICIARY NAME] met in [YEAR/LOCATION] and were married on [MARRIAGE DATE] in [MARRIAGE LOCATION]. 
[Include a brief 2-3 sentence narrative of their relationship timeline, living arrangements, and any children born to the marriage, using extracted facts].

II. EVIDENCE OF BONA FIDE MARRIAGE
The record establishes by a preponderance of the evidence that the marriage was entered into in good faith and not for the purpose of circumventing immigration laws. In support of the bona fide nature of the marriage, we enclose the following objective evidence:
- Certified Marriage Certificate
- [List specific joint financial documents, e.g. Joint Bank Account Statements]
- [List specific cohabitation documents, e.g. Joint Lease Agreement or Utility Bills]
- [List other evidence, e.g. Affidavits from family/friends, photographs, etc.]

III. ELIGIBILITY FOR ADJUSTMENT OF STATUS
[BENEFICIARY NAME] was last admitted to the United States on [ENTRY DATE] at [ENTRY LOCATION] on a [VISA STATUS] visa. The Beneficiary has maintained lawful presence and has not engaged in unauthorized employment or committed any acts that would render them inadmissible under INA § 212(a).

Furthermore, the Petitioner, [PETITIONER NAME], has submitted Form I-864, Affidavit of Support, demonstrating income that exceeds 125% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines, thereby establishing that the Beneficiary is not likely to become a public charge.

IV. CONCLUSION
Based on the enclosed forms, civil documents, and bona fide marriage evidence, the Petitioner respectfully requests the approval of the Form I-130 petition and the Beneficiary's Form I-485 adjustment of status application.

Respectfully submitted,
[ATTORNEY NAME OR PETITIONER NAME]

---

APPENDIX: EXHIBIT INDEX

Tab A - Required USCIS Forms and Filing Fees
- Form I-130 & I-130A
- Form I-485
- Form I-864
- Form I-765 & Form I-131 (if applicable)
- Form I-693 Medical Examination

Tab B - Civil Documents and Identity
- Petitioner's Proof of U.S. Citizenship (Passport/Birth Certificate)
- Beneficiary's Passport and Birth Certificate
- Beneficiary's Proof of Lawful Entry (I-94)
- Certified Marriage Certificate
- Proof of Termination of Prior Marriages (if applicable)

Tab C - Evidence of Bona Fide Marriage
- [List extracted joint financial docs]
- [List extracted cohabitation docs]
- [List photos and affidavits]
